3.name: Purifier's Endurance
3.html: You can reduce your Corruption Points by an additional 1 per short rest (in addition to the standard long rest reduction). Your Corruption Feature thresholds are shifted — you gain the first Corruption Feature at 33% instead of 25%, reducing the pace of accumulation somewhat. This does not reduce the eventual accumulation; it buys time.

6.name: Area Purification
6.html: Your Purification Touch extends to an area. As a 10-minute process, you can purify all creatures within 20 feet simultaneously, removing one contamination/curse from each. The Corruption Point cost is 1 per creature affected. You cannot purify yourself with this ability.

10.name: Unstoppable Service
10.html: Once per short rest, when your Corruption Points would exceed your maximum, they do not — instead, the excess is expelled as a burst of corrupt energy in a 10-foot radius (3d8 necrotic damage, Constitution save for half), and your Corruption Points remain at your maximum. This prevents the Corruption overload from affecting you once per rest.

14.name: The Burden Bearer
14.html:
Once per long rest, you can absorb one effect that is terminal or catastrophic from a creature within 30 feet: a disease that would kill them, a corruption that would destroy their Echomind, a spiritual contamination that would permanently alter them. The absorption is complete — the creature is freed from it entirely. You gain Corruption Points equal to the effect's severity (DM determines, minimum 10). If this drives you to your maximum, you make the resulting Constitution save at disadvantage, as the thing you absorbed was significant. The creature you saved from it is healed to full hit points from the relief.

---

system.description: The Purifier has made a vow that is not spoken in words but expressed in every action: they will absorb what is wrong so others don't have to. This is a complete spiritual and professional commitment that has no exit clause. Purifiers do not have a practice that ends when the situation improves. They are called to the places where the situation is worst, and they stay until it is better, and they leave carrying more than they arrived with. Every Purifier knows, eventually, that this trajectory only goes one direction. They do it anyway because they looked at the alternative — they looked at the children in the fracture zones, at the elders losing their minds to the Drift — and decided that their own trajectory was less important than the one they could change.
